comments.body: Does the same problem occur with Quarkus 2.12.2.Final and / or 2.13.0.CR1?
Could you see if this is similar github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/10716
With 2.12.2.Final of Quarkus I have the same error.
@Eusebio have you fixed that error? I am also facing the same error.

answers.body:
I had a similar or maybe the same issue and found a solution here https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/10716#issuecomment-1069943206. So in my issue I was creating a Flyway instance programmatically (Flyway.flyway()) and calling migrate(). This instantiation was the issue for me. Retrieving it via arc solved the issue. Since this is the number 1 thread to find when searching for this issue I'll leave this here.

QuarkusPathLocationScanner.setApplicationMigrationFiles(files);
DataSource ds = Flyway.configure().dataSource("jdbc:" + datasourceUrl, datasourceUsername, datasourcePassword).getDataSource();
FlywayContainerProducer flywayProducer = Arc.container().instance(FlywayContainerProducer.class).get();
 FlywayContainer flywayContainer = flywayProducer.createFlyway(ds, "<default>", true, true);
 Flyway flyway = flywayContainer.getFlyway();
 flyway.migrate();

I'm using the following config in my environment (and it works ;D) I just tried to use a different datasource for the migrations

quarkus.datasource.db-kind = postgresql
quarkus.datasource.username = postgres
quarkus.datasource.password = postgres
quarkus.datasource.reactive.url = vertx-reactive:postgresql://localhost:5432/mydb
quarkus.datasource.jdbc = false

quarkus.flyway."flyway-migration".active = true
quarkus.flyway."flyway-migration".migrate-at-start = true
quarkus.datasource."flyway-migration".db-kind = postgresql
quarkus.datasource."flyway-migration".jdbc.url = jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/mydb
quarkus.datasource."flyway-migration".username = postgres
quarkus.datasource."flyway-migration".password = postgres

If you are creating Flyway instance programmatically, make sure to add QuarkusFlywayClassProvider and QuarkusFlywayResourceProvider to avoid using the default scanner:

var flywayConfiguration = Flyway.configure();
var quarkusPathLocationScanner = new QuarkusPathLocationScanner(flywayConfiguration,
                                                    List.of(new Location("classpath:db/migration")));
flywayConfiguration.javaMigrationClassProvider(
                                    new QuarkusFlywayClassProvider<>(quarkusPathLocationScanner.scanForClasses()));
flywayConfiguration.resourceProvider(
                                    new QuarkusFlywayResourceProvider(quarkusPathLocationScanner.scanForResources()));
flywayConfiguration.encoding(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
flywayConfiguration.detectEncoding(false);
flywayConfiguration.failOnMissingLocations(false);
flywayConfiguration.dataSource(jdbcUrl, null, null);
var flyway = flywayConfiguration.load();

You also need to have at least one datasource configured in application.properties. For example, I am using SQLite (with DB files generated dynamically) and I configured the following:

# Without configuring quarkus.datasource.jdbc.url
quarkus.datasource.db-kind=sqlite
quarkus.datasource.jdbc.driver=org.sqlite.JDBC
quarkus.datasource.devservices.enabled=false

We needed this because FlywayProcessor will initialize QuarkusPathLocationScanner.applicationMigrationFiles and QuarkusPathLocationScanner.applicationMigrationClasses only if there is at least one datasource configured.

title: Quarkus compilation native fails with flyway
body:
I have this error but I cannot found any information about this:

2022-09-20 09:48:36,035 ERROR [io.qua.run.Application] (main) Failed to start application (with profile prod): java.lang.IllegalStateException: 'org.flywaydb.core.internal.scanner.Scanner' is never used in Quarkus
        at org.flywaydb.core.internal.scanner.Scanner.<init>(Scanner.java:27)
        at org.flywaydb.core.FlywayExecutor.createResourceAndClassProviders(FlywayExecutor.java:252)
        at org.flywaydb.core.FlywayExecutor.execute(FlywayExecutor.java:135)
        at org.flywaydb.core.Flyway.migrate(Flyway.java:124)

I'm pretty sure that the sql migration files are included with the compilation with the parameter "quarkus.native.resources.includes=db/migration/**".

Somebody have an idea that what is happening?

Versions:
Version info: 'GraalVM 22.1.0 Java 11 CE'
Quarkus 2.10.1

Compilation with maven:

mvn clean package -Dnative

I had testing also with versions of Quarkus 2.12.2.Final and 2.13.0.CR1 with the same results.
